text.title: Next.js App Router Patterns
text.summary: Segment structure, Server Actions, Route Handlers, middleware auth, and common anti-patterns in the App Router.

rule.statement: Every route segment should include `loading.tsx` (Suspense fallback) and `error.tsx` (error boundary). Do not delete them — they are required for graceful UX.
rule.rationale: Missing `loading.tsx` causes the entire segment to block until all data is resolved; missing `error.tsx` surfaces unhandled errors to users.

example.note: Server Component fetches session server-side. Each data section is independently wrapped in Suspense.
`'use server'` at the top of the file. Validate with Zod before any DB call. `revalidatePath` only for the affected route, not `'/'`.
Auth check before body parsing. Zod validation returns 400, not 500. Re-throws unknown errors for the error boundary.

section.title: Middleware

example.note: Middleware runs on Edge runtime — never import `crypto`, `fs`, or other Node-only modules here.

antipattern.reason: Middleware runs on the Edge runtime which does not support Node built-ins; the build fails silently or at runtime.
Over-invalidation purges the entire cache; performance collapses on high-traffic sites. Invalidate only the affected path or tag.

section.reference: Production next.config.ts with bundle analyzer, typed routes, security headers
